mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with persistent labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of months with PAYEMS growth > 150k, or correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) of more than 0.5% signals economic contraction that reduces equity valuations and forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,491.567 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2023-07-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 45% of quarters following GDPC1 contraction > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month while simultaneously the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points signals supply-side weakness coupled with labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days due to stagflationary pressures.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp within 1 month
failure_condition: Joint signal (INDPRO decline + UNRATE rise) produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month by more than 1.0% signals weakening consumer demand and reduced discretionary spending, which compresses corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 704,954 million dollars as of 2023-10-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of months following RSAFS decline > 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth exceeds 4.0% while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, real interest rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Joint signal (high inflation + low rates) produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining by more than 10% month-over-month signals deteriorating residential investment and forward demand weakness, which reduces construction employment and consumer wealth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,358.0 thousand units as of 2023-09-01
regime_hint: housing market downturn with credit tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of months following HOUST decline > 10%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously declines month-over-month, this dual signal indicates labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase with rising unemployment
failure_condition: Joint signal (high unemployment + payroll decline) produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year rolling windows.
